facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Archive annex lower record room behind the measure office stores in Brackenside, with hall review authority arriving upstairs

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Unranked, unlicensed salvage runner; shoulder still in sling and physically taxed but mobile; provisional notice retained and sealed deposition still pending; now armed with partial archival evidence that South Third’s hidden area ties to an old secondary bell conduit, a masonry patch, deferred reinforcement, dead resonance, and a transfer to a nonpublic vault index; emotionally focused, more secretive with allies, and newly alarmed that the hall or another institutional actor has begun searching records in parallel

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Stay ahead of Pike and the hall in the records race, trace the vault-index transfer tied to the sealed conduit and patched wall, preserve control of route knowledge, and keep the missing-men case from being folded into a neat hazard review

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Rowan still lacks money for the filing fee, has no license or sponsor, and cannot lawfully breach the South Third cordon; Pike is likely petitioning for probe access; hall review authority is now actively searching records; old maintenance materials are restricted; Benn and Derren remain missing; and the marker disturbance may indicate survivor movement, intrusion, or something worse below

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Lio remains an active strategic ally and now knows Rowan withheld the “older workmanship” detail from the hearing; Sena has peeled off to warn Benn’s sister about search-extension costs and remains a trusted outside ally; Darm Pell becomes a covert procedural contact who discreetly points Rowan toward restricted maintenance abstracts and warns about Sern and Pike; Archivist Nessa becomes a cautious information gatekeeper who allows limited access and hints at orphaned reference slates; Elias remains an off-page but strained family ally

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: The conflict escalates from public hearing fallout into a records-and-access race: Rowan must use fragmentary maintenance evidence to identify what was sealed in South Third before Pike, the guild side, or hall review authority can monopolize the answer, all while missing men remain below and official review crews prepare to define the truth without him
